section_1___details_of_applicant.principal_address: 4550 West Marquette Avenue, Suite 1200, Chicago, IL 60606, USA
section_1___details_of_applicant.nature_of_business:
Northbridge Global Components Inc. is a mid-sized multinational manufacturer and distributor of precision-engineered components used in industrial automation, transportation, and consumer electronics. The company designs and produces items such as custom fasteners, micro-molded plastic parts, and small metal assemblies that are sold primarily to OEMs (original equipment manufacturers). Operations include:

- Design and prototyping of mechanical and electromechanical components for automotive, rail, and aerospace suppliers.
- Manufacture of high-volume standardized fasteners and clips for consumer electronics, home appliances, and industrial machinery.
- Just-in-time distribution and inventory management services for large OEM customers, including vendor-managed inventory arrangements.
- Light assembly and kitting services at regional distribution centers in the USA, Canada, Germany, and Singapore.

The business model combines in-house manufacturing at plants in the USA, Mexico, and Poland with a network of qualified contract manufacturers in Asia-Pacific. Northbridge sells primarily on a B2B basis under long-term supply contracts, with annual quality audits and strict supply-chain compliance requirements. The company places particular emphasis on quality control, traceability of materials, and secure handling of customer tooling and IP, which is one of the reasons it is seeking robust commercial crime insurance coverage.
section_1___details_of_applicant.details_of_any_acquisitions_or_mergers_s:
Since the last published audited accounts (fiscal year ended 2024-12-31), Northbridge Global Components Inc. has completed one acquisition and one small asset purchase:

1) Acquisition of PrecisionFast Europe Sp. z o.o. (completed 2025-03-15):
   - Jurisdiction: Poland, with sales offices in Germany and the Netherlands.
   - Business: Manufacturer of specialty fasteners for rail and heavy industry.
   - Transaction Type: 100% share purchase, now a wholly owned subsidiary of Northbridge Global Components Inc.
   - Integration Status: Finance, HR, and IT systems have been largely integrated into Northbridge’s global ERP (SAP S/4HANA) environment as of 2025-07-01. Internal controls over payments, vendor onboarding, and inventory management have been aligned with Northbridge’s global policies, including dual-authorization for wire transfers and quarterly internal audits.
   - Employee Impact: Approximately 85 employees (including production and administrative staff) joined the Northbridge group through this acquisition.

2) Asset purchase of tooling and inventory from Alpine MicroMold GmbH (completed 2025-01-22):
   - Jurisdiction: Germany.
   - Business: Tooling and select customer contracts relating to micro-molded plastic components for medical devices and consumer electronics.
   - Transaction Type: Asset purchase; no transfer of corporate entity. A small team of 12 engineering and sales staff were offered employment with Northbridge’s German subsidiary, Northbridge Components Deutschland GmbH.
   - Integration Status: Tooling and inventory have been physically relocated to Northbridge’s facility in Stuttgart, Germany. Staff have been onboarded under Northbridge’s standard HR, payroll, and compliance procedures, including background checks for employees in sensitive finance and inventory-control roles.

No mergers, spin-offs, or divestitures have taken place during the same period. All new entities and employees brought in through these transactions are intended to be included within the scope of the requested commercial crime coverage, and Northbridge’s internal audit team has conducted initial risk assessments and implemented consistent anti-fraud and segregation-of-duties controls at these locations.

geographic_split_of_operations.canada_locations:
Primary Canadian operations are conducted through Northbridge Components Canada Ltd., with the following locations:
- Toronto, Ontario (Head office and sales; ~35 employees) – 180 King Street West, Suite 2300, Toronto, ON M5H 3T9.
- Brampton, Ontario (Regional distribution center and light assembly; ~40 employees) – 10225 Airport Road, Brampton, ON L6S 0C5.
- Montreal, Quebec (Sales office and customer support; ~20 employees) – 600 Boulevard de Maisonneuve Ouest, Suite 1500, Montreal, QC H3A 3J2.
geographic_split_of_operations.uk_locations:
UK operations are handled by Northbridge Components UK Ltd. and focus primarily on sales, engineering support, and vendor-managed inventory services:
- Birmingham, West Midlands (Regional office and small warehouse; ~40 employees) – 12 Colmore Row, Birmingham B3 2QD.
- Reading, Berkshire (Sales and technical support office; ~20 employees) – 250 Brook Drive, Green Park, Reading RG2 6UA.
geographic_split_of_operations.europe_locations:
Continental European operations (excluding the UK) are conducted via several subsidiaries, primarily involved in manufacturing, light assembly, and regional distribution:
- Stuttgart, Germany (~70 employees) – Northbridge Components Deutschland GmbH, manufacturing and engineering center, located at Breitwiesenstrasse 18, 70565 Stuttgart. This site produces precision metal components and houses a small R&D team.
- Wroclaw, Poland (~85 employees) – PrecisionFast Europe Sp. z o.o. (recently acquired), manufacturing facility and warehouse at Ul. Robotnicza 45, 53-608 Wroclaw, specializing in heavy-duty fasteners for rail and heavy equipment.
- Eindhoven, Netherlands (~25 employees) – Sales and logistics hub at Flight Forum 120, 5657 DD Eindhoven, supporting Benelux and Nordic customers with regional inventory and just-in-time deliveries.
- Lyon, France (~30 employees) – Sales office and technical application support center at 15 Quai Charles de Gaulle, 69006 Lyon, focusing on automotive and industrial OEM accounts.
geographic_split_of_operations.usa_locations:
The United States is Northbridge’s largest region by headcount and revenue, with multiple facilities covering corporate functions, manufacturing, and distribution:
- Chicago, Illinois (~160 employees) – Global headquarters, finance, HR, and central engineering located at 4550 West Marquette Avenue, Suite 1200, Chicago, IL 60606. This office also houses the central risk management and internal audit teams.
- Elgin, Illinois (~110 employees) – Primary US manufacturing plant for precision metal components and light assemblies at 2100 Randall Road, Elgin, IL 60123.
- Dallas, Texas (~80 employees) – Regional distribution center and customer service hub at 9750 North Royal Lane, Suite 400, Irving, TX 75063, supporting customers in the Southern US.
- Reno, Nevada (~50 employees) – West Coast warehouse and light assembly facility at 6450 Double R Boulevard, Reno, NV 89511, focusing on consumer electronics customers.
- Detroit, Michigan (~30 employees) – Automotive-focused sales and engineering support office at 300 Renaissance Center, Suite 2200, Detroit, MI 48243.
geographic_split_of_operations.asia_pacific_locations:
Asia-Pacific operations primarily support sourcing, quality assurance for contract manufacturers, and regional distribution:
- Singapore (~65 employees) – Regional headquarters and distribution hub at 8 Shenton Way, #34-02 AXA Tower, Singapore 068811. Functions include regional management, supply-chain coordination, and quality control.
- Shenzhen, China (~45 employees) – Sourcing, supplier quality engineering, and liaison office at 9F, Block B, Nanshan Software Park, 1001 Software Avenue, Nanshan District, Shenzhen, Guangdong 518057.
- Penang, Malaysia (~25 employees) – Light assembly and kitting facility at 2 Jalan Batu Uban, 11700 Gelugor, Penang, serving electronics manufacturers in Malaysia and Thailand.
- Sydney, Australia (~20 employees) – Sales and customer support office at Level 18, 1 Market Street, Sydney NSW 2000, mainly supporting industrial and infrastructure customers in Australia and New Zealand.
geographic_split_of_operations.other_locations:
Other regions consist primarily of smaller sales and representative offices that support local customers but do not involve manufacturing:
- Mexico City, Mexico (~20 employees) – Sales and technical service office at Torre Reforma, Piso 14, Paseo de la Reforma 483, Cuauhtémoc, 06500 Ciudad de México.
- Sao Paulo, Brazil (~15 employees) – Regional sales office at Av. Paulista, 1374, 9º Andar, Bela Vista, São Paulo – SP, 01310-100.
- Johannesburg, South Africa (~15 employees) – Sales and customer support office at 1st Floor, 39 Melrose Boulevard, Melrose Arch, Johannesburg 2196.
geographic_split_of_operations.total_locations:
Northbridge Global Components Inc. and its subsidiaries operate across multiple regions with a combined headcount of approximately 1,000 employees. The principal operational locations are:

North America:
- USA:
  • Chicago, IL – Global headquarters and corporate functions.
  • Elgin, IL – Major manufacturing plant.
  • Dallas (Irving), TX – Distribution center and customer service.
  • Reno, NV – West Coast warehouse and light assembly.
  • Detroit, MI – Automotive-focused sales and engineering.
- Canada:
  • Toronto, ON – Canadian head office and sales.
  • Brampton, ON – Distribution and light assembly.
  • Montreal, QC – Sales and customer support.

Europe (excluding UK):
- Stuttgart, Germany – Manufacturing and engineering center.
- Wroclaw, Poland – Heavy-duty fasteners manufacturing plant.
- Eindhoven, Netherlands – Sales and logistics hub.
- Lyon, France – Sales and technical support center.

United Kingdom:
- Birmingham – Regional office, small warehouse, and customer support.
- Reading – Sales and technical support office.

Asia-Pacific:
- Singapore – Regional HQ and distribution hub.
- Shenzhen, China – Sourcing and supplier quality office.
- Penang, Malaysia – Light assembly and kitting facility.
- Sydney, Australia – Sales and customer support office.

Other Regions:
- Mexico City, Mexico – Sales and technical service office.
- Sao Paulo, Brazil – Regional sales office.
- Johannesburg, South Africa – Sales and customer support office.

These combined locations reflect the current global operating footprint for which commercial crime insurance coverage is being sought, including all manufacturing plants, warehouses, and offices where employees handle cash, inventory, accounts receivable, payments, or other assets susceptible to fraud or dishonesty.
